papers.title: Read It Back: Pretrained MLLMs Are Zero-Shot Reward Models for Text-to-Image Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract  In this paper, we propose SpectraReward, a training-free reward function that turns pretrained MLLMs into off-the-shelf reward models for image-generation reinforcement learning. Instead of asking the MLLM to judge a generated image or answer decomposed verification questions, SpectraReward measures how well the original prompt can be recovered from the generated image through a single image-conditioned, teacher-forced forward pass. We use the average image-conditioned prompt log-likelihood as the reward, directly reusing the MLLM's pretrained image-text alignment ability without preference labels, reward-model fine-tuning. We further introduce Self-SpectraReward, a special case for unified multimodal models where the policy's own understanding branch serves as the reward model for its generation branch, forming a closed-loop self-improving framework without external reward models or external knowledge. Extensive experiments validate SpectraReward through a broad image-generation RL study covering two diffusion models, three RL algorithms, nine reward MLLM backbones from four MLLM families spanning 4B to 235B parameters, and five out-of-distribution text-to-image benchmarks. Results show that both SpectraReward and Self-SpectraReward significantly and consistently improve generation performance and outperform prior MLLM-derived reward training methods. Further analysis reveals that larger reward MLLMs are not always better, while Self-SpectraReward can match or surpass much larger external reward models, suggesting that reward-policy alignment is a key factor for effective image-generation RL. Project Page: https://huangrh99.github.io/SpectraReward/

ru.title: Замкнутый цикл самооценки для улучшения генерации изображений
ru.desc: В статье предлагается SpectraReward — функция награды, которая превращает предварительно обученные мультимодальные большие языковые модели в готовые к использованию модели оценки для обучения с подкреплением генерации изображений. Вместо того чтобы просить модель судить о сгенерированном изображении, метод измеряет качество восстановления исходного текстового запроса из изображения через прямой проход модели кондиционирования, используя среднее логарифмическое правдоподобие как награду. Авторы также предлагают Self-SpectraReward — специальный случай для унифицированных мультимодальных моделей, где ветвь понимания политики служит моделью оценки для её ветви генерации, создавая замкнутый цикл самосовершенствования без внешних моделей награды. Экспериментальная валидация охватывает две диффузионные модели, три алгоритма RL и девять мультимодальных моделей различных семейств, демонстрируя значительные улучшения и превосходство над существующими методами на основе мультимодальных моделей.

en.title: Transforming MLLMs into Reward Models for Enhanced Image Generation
en.desc: This paper introduces SpectraReward, a novel reward function that allows pretrained Multimodal Language Models (MLLMs) to be used as reward models in image generation tasks without additional training. Instead of evaluating generated images directly, SpectraReward assesses how well the original text prompt can be reconstructed from the generated image, using the log-likelihood of the prompt as the reward signal. The authors also present Self-SpectraReward, which utilizes the model's own understanding to create a self-improving feedback loop for image generation. Experimental results demonstrate that both SpectraReward and Self-SpectraReward enhance image generation performance significantly, outperforming traditional reward training methods and highlighting the importance of reward-policy alignment.

zh.title: SpectraReward：无训练的图像生成奖励模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为SpectraReward的无训练奖励函数，旨在将预训练的多模态大语言模型（MLLM）转化为图像生成强化学习的即用奖励模型。SpectraReward通过单次图像条件的前向传播，测量生成图像与原始提示的恢复程度，而不是让MLLM判断生成的图像或回答分解的验证问题。我们使用图像条件下的提示对数似然平均值作为奖励，直接利用MLLM的预训练图像-文本对齐能力，而无需偏好标签或奖励模型微调。此外，我们引入了Self-SpectraReward，作为统一多模态模型的特殊情况，形成一个闭环自我改进框架，进一步验证了SpectraReward在图像生成强化学习中的有效性。

papers.title: SynthDocBench: Controlled Benchmark for Long-Context Visual Document Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract  Vision language models (VLMs) have achieved strong performance on visual document understanding benchmarks such as DocVQA, ChartQA, and MMLongBench-Doc. However, real-world documents combine multiple factors such as length, layout complexity, modality, and question difficulty, which makes it difficult to attribute model failures to specific causes. We introduce SynthDocBench, a fully synthetic benchmark for long-context visual document understanding that systematically controls factors including document length, layout structure, modality composition, and question type. The benchmark is constructed using a combinatorial design, each factor is varied independently across generated documents, enabling controlled analysis of model behavior. Documents are generated end to end using an LLM pipeline across six layout archetypes, with a 40 percent random override to prevent models from exploiting spurious correlations. Additionally, SynthDocBench spans long-context documents with substantially greater length and structural diversity than existing benchmarks. Evaluating seven frontier VLMs, we uncover three failure modes that existing benchmarks cannot surface: sharp degradation with document length, a systematic positional sensitivity in which the middle third of a document is hardest for five of six models and five of six models show a negative Early-to-Late trend (steepest decline: 8.3 percentage points), and breakdown of chart comprehension in long-document settings. These results suggest that current models may be overfitting to benchmark artifacts rather than achieving robust long-context visual document understanding.

ru.title: Контролируемый анализ слабостей VLM в понимании длинных документов через синтетический бенчмарк
ru.desc: В работе представлен SynthDocBench — синтетический бенчмарк для оценки моделей зрения и языка (VLM) на задачах понимания длинных документов. Бенчмарк использует комбинаторный дизайн, систематически варьируя факторы, такие как длина документа, структура макета, модальность и тип вопроса, что позволяет контролируемо анализировать поведение моделей. Авторы выявили три критических режима отказа VLM: деградация производительности с увеличением длины документа, позиционная чувствительность (средняя часть документа самая сложная) и снижение качества понимания графиков в длинных документах. Полученные результаты свидетельствуют, что существующие модели переобучаются на артефактах бенчмарков вместо того, чтобы развивать устойчивое понимание длинного контекста.

en.title: Unveiling Model Weaknesses in Visual Document Understanding
en.desc: This paper introduces SynthDocBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate vision language models (VLMs) on long-context visual document understanding. It systematically varies factors like document length, layout complexity, and question types to analyze model performance more effectively. The benchmark uses a combinatorial design to generate synthetic documents, allowing for controlled testing of VLMs. The study reveals critical failure modes in existing models, indicating that they may be overfitting to previous benchmarks rather than truly understanding complex documents.

zh.title: 揭示视觉语言模型的潜在缺陷
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新的基准测试工具SynthDocBench，用于评估视觉语言模型在长文本视觉文档理解中的表现。该基准通过系统控制文档长度、布局结构、模态组合和问题类型等因素，帮助分析模型的行为。研究发现，现有模型在处理长文档时存在三种失败模式，包括文档长度导致的性能急剧下降和对文档中间部分的敏感性。结果表明，当前模型可能过度拟合基准测试的特征，而未能实现稳健的长文本视觉文档理解。

papers.title: Blind-Spots-Bench: Evaluating Blind Spots in Multimodal Models
papers.abstract: Abstract  Modern AI models achieve strong performance on many established benchmarks, yet they still fail on tasks that humans find almost trivial, such as manipulating a string or drawing a dog with five legs. These examples suggest that existing benchmarks may under-measure persistent blind spots in current systems. We introduce blind-spots-bench, a benchmark designed to expose such blind spots through tasks that appear simple for humans but remain challenging for modern AI. We collect raw questions from students in an AI course, clean and annotate them with structured reference solutions, and propose a task taxonomy tailored to the resulting dataset of 235 samples. We further develop an automated grading pipeline to evaluate a wide range of models, including open-weight and closed-source language, vision-language, and image-generation models. Our analysis on blind-spots-bench reveals that closed-source frontier models can substantially outperform open-weight models with even approx10% gap, even when they attain comparable performance on existing benchmarks. A more fine-grained analysis shows that no single model dominates across all task types, and that some tasks remain challenging for all evaluated models. These results highlight the value of blind-spots-bench as a diagnostic stress test for identifying concrete weaknesses in current modern models.
affiliations: Ecole Polytechnique Federale de Lausanne (EPFL), Switzerland

ru.title: Выявление скрытых ограничений современных AI моделей через специализированный бенчмарк
ru.desc: В статье представлена новая бенчмарк-задача blind-spots-bench, которая выявляет слепые пятна современных моделей ИИ через простые для человека, но сложные для моделей задачи. Исследователи собрали 235 образцов вопросов от студентов, разработали структурированную таксономию задач и создали автоматизированный конвейер оценки для тестирования различных моделей, включая открытые и закрытые language models, vision-language и image-generation модели. Анализ показал значительный разрыв (около 10%) между закрытыми frontier-моделями и открытыми моделями, несмотря на сравнимую производительность на существующих бенчмарках. Результаты демонстрируют ценность blind-spots-bench как диагностического инструмента для выявления конкретных слабостей современных AI систем.

en.title: Uncovering AI's Hidden Weaknesses with Blind-Spots-Bench
en.desc: This paper introduces a new benchmark called blind-spots-bench, aimed at identifying weaknesses in modern AI models that are not captured by traditional benchmarks. The benchmark consists of tasks that are simple for humans but difficult for AI, revealing persistent blind spots in model performance. The authors collected and annotated a dataset of 235 samples from AI students, creating a structured task taxonomy for evaluation. Their findings indicate that closed-source models significantly outperform open-weight models on these tasks, emphasizing the need for more comprehensive testing to understand AI limitations.

zh.title: 揭示AI模型的盲点
zh.desc: 现代人工智能模型在许多基准测试中表现出色，但在一些人类认为简单的任务上仍然失败，例如操作字符串或画一只五条腿的狗。这表明现有的基准测试可能无法充分测量当前系统的盲点。我们提出了blind-spots-bench，这是一个旨在通过看似简单但对现代AI仍具挑战性的任务来揭示这些盲点的基准测试。我们的分析显示，封闭源模型在某些任务上显著优于开放源模型，且没有单一模型在所有任务类型上占据主导地位。

papers.title: Search Beyond What Can Be Taught: Evolving the Knowledge Boundary in Agentic Visual Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract  Visual generators excel at rendering, but they confidently fabricate what they do not know. User requests are unbounded, evolving, and deeply long-tailed: new characters, trending entities, post-cutoff events, and more. This world-knowledge bottleneck is structural: generators are trained on fixed corpora, but the visual world is open-ended. We construct SearchGen-20K and SearchGen-Bench, with 20,839 prompts spanning twelve failure categories and twenty-two domains, paired with a pre-executed multimodal SearchGen-Corpus-1M to support offline, reproducible research. On SearchGen-Bench, frontier open generators score only 21 to 28 out of 100, a 40-point collapse invisible to existing benchmarks. The natural remedy is to employ search tools, enabling agentic visual generation. However, we find that naive search fails: it retrieves indiscriminately, injecting noise into prompts the generator already handles. We trace the root cause to a generator-specific, evolving knowledge boundary: the divide between what a generator can internalize through training and what must remain in external context. Although this boundary is hard to specify in advance, we show that it is discoverable through a teach-then-search co-training framework. Even a minimal version of this co-training recipe produces monotonic improvement, laying the foundation for recursive self-improvement in visual generation that can meet world-knowledge-grounded requests. We release the full dataset, co-training corpus, and search corpus as a replayable harness for tool-augmented, world-knowledge-grounded visual generation.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Поиск в помощь памяти: визуальная генерация с внешним знанием мира
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема визуальных генераторов, которые не могут синтезировать изображения объектов и событий, отсутствующих в их тренировочных данных. Авторы создали бенчмарк SearchGen-Bench с 20,839 промптами, выявив критическое падение качества современных генераторов до 21-28 баллов из 100. Предложено решение на основе агентного подхода, объединяющего поиск информации с генерацией через框架co-training, который позволяет модели различать границу своих знаний и определять, когда требуется внешний контекст. Такой метод обеспечивает монотонное улучшение качества и закладывает основу для рекурсивной самооптимизации в мультимодальной генерации, способной работать с запросами на знание реального мира.

en.title: Bridging Knowledge Gaps in Visual Generation
en.desc: This paper addresses the limitations of visual generators that often create inaccurate content when faced with unfamiliar requests. It introduces SearchGen-20K and SearchGen-Bench, which consist of a large set of prompts designed to evaluate the performance of these generators across various domains. The authors identify a knowledge boundary that restricts generators from accessing up-to-date information, leading to poor performance on new or evolving topics. To overcome this, they propose a teach-then-search co-training framework that enhances the generator's ability to incorporate external knowledge, resulting in improved visual generation capabilities.

zh.title: 突破知识瓶颈，实现智能视觉生成
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了视觉生成模型在处理用户请求时的局限性，尤其是在面对未知信息时的自信错误。作者提出了SearchGen-20K和SearchGen-Bench这两个数据集，旨在评估生成模型在不同领域和失败类别中的表现。研究发现，现有的生成模型在这些基准测试中得分较低，显示出知识瓶颈的问题。为了解决这个问题，论文提出了一种教-再搜索的共同训练框架，能够帮助生成模型逐步改善其知识边界，从而更好地满足用户的请求。

papers.title: Know Before Fix: QA-Driven Repository Knowledge Acquisition for Software Issue Resolution
papers.abstract: Abstract  LLM-based coding agents have significantly advanced automated software issue resolution, yet they remain highly prone to factual errors caused by insufficient repository understanding. Recent methods attempt to mitigate this limitation through pre-repair repository exploration; however, their fix-driven strategies explore repositories without identifying the agent's knowledge gaps, often yielding imprecise context that fails to bridge the underlying understanding deficit. In this paper, we propose ACQUIRE, a QA-driven framework for software issue resolution. Mirroring how experienced developers first comprehend unfamiliar code before attempting a fix, ACQUIRE explicitly acquires repository knowledge prior to repair. The framework decouples knowledge acquisition from patch generation through two stages: in the first stage, a Questioner and an Answerer collaborate to acquire structured repository knowledge, where the Questioner poses targeted questions and the Answerer produces evidence-grounded answers through autonomous exploration; in the second stage, the Resolver leverages the resulting QA knowledge to generate informed patches. By transforming implicit knowledge gaps into explicit, factually reliable understanding, ACQUIRE accelerates knowledge-intensive repair stages and enables more accurate resolution. Experiments on SWE-bench Verified demonstrate that ACQUIRE consistently outperforms representative pre-repair methods, raising Pass@1 by up to 4.4 percentage points with modest additional cost and time.

ru.title: Спросить перед тем, как чинить: явное изучение кода вместо слепого поиска
ru.desc: Статья описывает ACQUIRE — фреймворк на основе больших языковых моделей для разрешения ошибок в коде. Вместо того чтобы сразу генерировать исправления, система сначала явно изучает репозиторий через диалог вопрос-ответ, где специальный модуль задаёт целевые вопросы, а другой модуль находит ответы путём автоматического исследования кода. Затем третий компонент использует полученные знания для генерации точных патчей. Такой подход трансформирует неявные пробелы в знаниях в явное и надёжное понимание, что приводит к более точному разрешению проблем и улучшает результаты на 4.4 процентных пункта.

en.title: ACQUIRE: Bridging Knowledge Gaps for Better Software Fixes
en.desc: This paper introduces ACQUIRE, a framework designed to improve automated software issue resolution by addressing the knowledge gaps of coding agents. Unlike previous methods that explore repositories without understanding the agent's limitations, ACQUIRE uses a two-stage process to first gather structured knowledge through a Questioner and Answerer system. This approach allows the agent to gain a clear understanding of the code before attempting to generate fixes. The results show that ACQUIRE significantly enhances the accuracy of software repairs, outperforming existing methods in terms of effectiveness while maintaining reasonable resource usage.

zh.title: ACQUIRE：提升软件问题解决的知识获取框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为ACQUIRE的框架，用于软件问题的解决。该框架通过问答驱动的方式，首先获取代码库的知识，然后再进行修复。ACQUIRE分为两个阶段：第一阶段，提问者和回答者合作获取结构化的知识；第二阶段，解决者利用这些知识生成修复补丁。实验结果表明，ACQUIRE在准确性和效率上优于现有的预修复方法。

papers.title: MuScriptor: An Open Model for Multi-Instrument Music Transcription
papers.abstract: Abstract  Existing methods for automatic music transcription are often limited to single-instrument recordings or fail on complex, real music mixes. Although previous work utilizes synthetic training data, the resulting models generalize poorly, leading to largely unusable transcription output in realistic, multi-instrument settings. In this work, we analyze the effectiveness of synthetic data for pre-training while combining it with fine-tuning on real music audio and post-training using reinforcement learning. We further introduce conditioning on instrument presence to customize transcriptions. Finally, we release MuScriptor, an open-weight multi-instrument music transcription model that works on real-world music recordings from across a diverse range of musical genres.

ru.title: Синтез и обучение с подкреплением для расшифровки реальной многоинструментальной музыки
ru.desc: В работе решается задача автоматической расшифровки музыки для многоинструментальных записей путём комбинирования предварительного обучения на синтетических данных с дальнейшей тонкой настройкой на реальной музыке и использованием обучения с подкреплением. Авторы вводят механизм обусловливания наличием инструментов, позволяющий кастомизировать получаемые расшифровки под конкретный состав. Предложенный подход значительно улучшает обобщаемость модели, решая проблему плохого переноса с синтетических данных на реальную музыку. Исследователи выпускают открытую модель MuScriptor, которая успешно работает на многоинструментальных записях из разнообразных музыкальных жанров.

en.title: Transforming Music Transcription with Real-World Data and Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges of automatic music transcription, particularly in complex, multi-instrument recordings. It critiques existing methods that rely heavily on synthetic data, which often do not perform well in real-world scenarios. The authors propose a novel approach that combines synthetic pre-training with fine-tuning on actual music audio and reinforcement learning for improved accuracy. They also introduce MuScriptor, a versatile model designed to handle diverse musical genres and instrument combinations effectively.

zh.title: MuScriptor：多乐器音乐转录的新突破
zh.desc: 现有的自动音乐转录方法通常仅限于单一乐器录音，或者在复杂的真实音乐混合中表现不佳。尽管之前的研究利用了合成训练数据，但生成的模型在真实的多乐器环境中泛化能力较差，导致转录输出几乎无法使用。本文分析了合成数据在预训练中的有效性，并结合真实音乐音频进行微调，以及使用强化学习进行后训练。我们还引入了乐器存在的条件，以定制转录，最终发布了MuScriptor，这是一个开放权重的多乐器音乐转录模型，能够处理来自多种音乐风格的真实音乐录音。

papers.title: Let RGB Be the Language of Vision
papers.abstract: Abstract  This work introduces a unified formulation for vision models, where diverse forms of visual information beyond natural images, such as masks, depth maps, and other structured visual signals, are all represented as RGB images, while general visual tasks can be converted into a common RGB-to-RGB image editing problem. In this paradigm, different types of visual information internally share the same encoding and decoding architecture and parameters as natural images, enabling a single model to transfer across tasks through a unified visual interface, in a way analogous to how language models operate over text. We refer to this formulation as RGB In and RGB Out (RINO). Built upon a generic image editing backbone without task-specific fine-tuning, RINO demonstrates robust and competitive zero-shot performance on both dense understanding tasks such as segmentation and depth estimation (where we unify outputs as RGB), and dense-conditioned generation tasks such as pose-to-image generation (where we unify inputs as RGB). We hope this study provides useful insights toward general unified vision-language systems, where diverse visual tasks can be expressed, interpreted, and solved through a shared visual language. Code is available at https://github.com/yangtiming/RINO.

ru.title: Один язык для всех визуальных задач: унифицированное видение через RGB
ru.desc: Работа предлагает унифицированный подход к видению, где все типы визуальной информации (маски, карты глубины, структурные сигналы) представляются в виде RGB-изображений, а различные задачи обработки изображений сводятся к единой задаче редактирования изображения RGB-to-RGB. Модель использует одну общую архитектуру энкодера-декодера для всех типов данных, что позволяет одной модели решать разные задачи через унифицированный визуальный интерфейс, аналогично тому, как языковые модели работают с текстом. RINO демонстрирует конкурентоспособную zero-shot производительность как на задачах плотного понимания сцены (сегментация, оценка глубины), так и на задачах условной генерации (трансформация позы в изображение) без специализированной дообучения. Подход открывает путь к созданию единых мультимодальных систем, где различные визуальные задачи решаются через общий визуальный язык.

en.title: Unified Vision Processing with RINO: One Model, Many Tasks
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach called RGB In and RGB Out (RINO) for handling various types of visual information, such as masks and depth maps, by treating them all as RGB images. By using a common encoding and decoding architecture, RINO allows a single model to perform multiple visual tasks without needing specific adjustments for each task. This method achieves strong performance in both understanding tasks like segmentation and generation tasks like pose-to-image conversion, all while maintaining a unified visual interface. The goal is to advance the development of general vision-language systems that can interpret and solve diverse visual challenges using a shared framework.

zh.title: 统一视觉模型：RGB输入输出的创新
zh.desc: 这项工作提出了一种统一的视觉模型框架，将多种视觉信息形式（如掩码、深度图和其他结构化视觉信号）都表示为RGB图像。通过这种方式，通用视觉任务可以转化为一个共同的RGB到RGB的图像编辑问题。不同类型的视觉信息共享相同的编码和解码架构，使得单一模型能够在任务之间进行迁移，类似于语言模型在文本上的操作。我们称这种框架为RGB输入和RGB输出（RINO），它在密集理解和生成任务上展示了强大的零-shot性能。

papers.title: MonkeyOCRv2: A Visual-Text Foundation Model for Document AI
papers.abstract: Abstract  Mainstream visual encoders are pretrained on natural images and cannot be effectively applied to document images without document-oriented adaptation, as dense text and fine-grained character strokes demand character-level visual perception. We present MonkeyOCRv2, a visual-text pretrained model for document AI. First, we construct MonkeyDoc v2, to our knowledge the largest document-image pretraining corpus, comprising 113 million images spanning 17 languages. Second, we propose a pretraining strategy that jointly learns image-to-text generation and pixel-level document reconstruction: the former aligns visual representations with textual content, while the latter preserves character strokes and layout details. Extensive experiments are conducted on five representative document analysis tasks, including text recognition, formula recognition, text detection, document tampering detection, and overlapping text segmentation. Replacing the original encoders with MonkeyOCRv2 consistently improves performance across all five tasks. Finally, we validate its effectiveness as the vision encoder of multimodal large language models on the more challenging tasks of document parsing and document understanding. Kept frozen and paired with a lightweight language model, it yields a 0.7B document parsing model that sets a new open-source state-of-the-art on MDPBench, a recent benchmark spanning digital-born and photographed documents across 17 languages, surpassing the previous best 3B dots.mocr by 2.8% absolute with a vision encoder roughly 11times smaller. The frozen encoder also powers a document understanding model that outperforms counterparts built on CLIP, DINO, and SAM across eight benchmarks under identical training settings. These results suggest that document-oriented visual pretraining can serve as a foundation for document intelligence in its own right.
affiliations: Huazhong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Документо-ориентированное предобучение как основа интеллекта для работы с документами
ru.desc: В статье представлена MonkeyOCRv2 — визуально-текстовая модель, предварительно обученная на документах для задач анализа документов. Авторы создали MonkeyDoc v2, крупнейший корпус из 113 миллионов изображений документов на 17 языках, и предложили стратегию предобучения, которая совместно оптимизирует генерацию текста из изображения и реконструкцию пиксель-уровня документа. Модель демонстрирует значительные улучшения на пяти задачах анализа документов: распознавание текста, распознавание формул, детекция текста, выявление подделок и сегментация перекрывающегося текста. Когда модель используется как визуальный энкодер в многомодальных больших языковых моделях, она позволяет создать компактный 0.7B парсер документов, который превосходит предыдущие решения при меньшем размере.

en.title: Revolutionizing Document AI with MonkeyOCRv2
en.desc: This paper introduces MonkeyOCRv2, a visual-text pretrained model specifically designed for document images, addressing the limitations of traditional visual encoders that are trained on natural images. The authors create MonkeyDoc v2, a large dataset of 113 million document images in 17 languages, to enhance the model's training. They propose a dual pretraining strategy that focuses on both image-to-text generation and pixel-level document reconstruction, ensuring that the model captures fine details like character strokes and layout. The results show that MonkeyOCRv2 significantly improves performance on various document analysis tasks and sets a new benchmark for document parsing and understanding.

zh.title: 文档智能的新基础：MonkeyOCRv2
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了MonkeyOCRv2，这是一个针对文档图像的视觉文本预训练模型。我们构建了MonkeyDoc v2，这是迄今为止最大的文档图像预训练语料库，包含1.13亿张图像，涵盖17种语言。该模型采用了一种联合学习图像到文本生成和像素级文档重建的预训练策略，从而提高了文本识别、公式识别等五个文档分析任务的性能。实验结果表明，MonkeyOCRv2在文档解析和理解任务中表现优异，超越了现有的最佳模型。

papers.title: Towards Autonomous and Auditable Medical Imaging Model Development
papers.abstract: Abstract  Large language model (LLM) agents are beginning to automate machine learning engineering (MLE) by coupling planning, code execution, debugging, and empirical feedback. Translating this capability to medical imaging remains difficult because each task imposes modality-specific experimentation and strict requirements for validation protocols and prediction artifacts. Here we introduce AMID, an autonomous multi-agent framework for medical imaging model development. AMID first proposes Data-Conditioned Method Planning, which refines coarse task-level search spaces into executable, parallelizable method lanes grounded in task-specific data analysis and runnable medical-imaging resources. It then develops Verification-Guided Two-Stage Optimization, moving from broad early exploration of diverse method lanes to selective exploitation of promising candidates while enforcing strict verification of validation protocols, metric computation, and prediction artifacts throughout the optimization. Across 20 medical imaging challenge tasks spanning diverse modalities and prediction types, AMID outperformed evaluated general-purpose MLE systems and, on several tasks, approached or matched strong human-designed challenge solutions. These results suggest that AMID can turn task-specific medical imaging model development from bespoke manual engineering into an agentic workflow for producing high-performing and auditable model artifacts across heterogeneous tasks.
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Агентная система для автоматизации разработки моделей медицинской визуализации
ru.desc: В статье представлена AMID — автономная мультиагентная система, которая использует LLM для автоматизации разработки моделей в медицинской визуализации. Система применяет метод Data-Conditioned Method Planning, который адаптирует пространство поиска методов под специфику конкретной задачи и доступных данных. Затем используется Verification-Guided Two-Stage Optimization, которая сначала исследует различные подходы, а затем фокусируется на самых перспективных кандидатах с строгой проверкой валидации. На 20 различных медицинских задачах AMID показала результаты, сравнимые с ручной разработкой экспертов и превосходящие существующие системы автоматизации машинного обучения.

en.title: Automating Medical Imaging Model Development with AMID
en.desc: This paper presents AMID, an autonomous framework designed to enhance the development of medical imaging models using large language model (LLM) agents. AMID employs Data-Conditioned Method Planning to streamline the process by refining task-level searches into actionable methods based on specific data analysis. It also incorporates Verification-Guided Two-Stage Optimization, which balances exploration of various methods with a focus on validating results and metrics. The framework demonstrated superior performance over traditional machine learning engineering systems across multiple medical imaging tasks, indicating its potential to automate and improve model development in this field.

zh.title: AMID：医学影像模型开发的智能化新方法
zh.desc: 大型语言模型（LLM）代理开始通过规划、代码执行、调试和经验反馈来自动化机器学习工程（MLE）。将这种能力应用于医学影像仍然面临挑战，因为每个任务都有特定的实验要求和严格的验证协议。本文介绍了AMID，一个用于医学影像模型开发的自主多代理框架。AMID通过数据条件的方法规划和验证引导的两阶段优化，显著提高了医学影像任务的模型开发效率，超越了传统的通用MLE系统。

papers.title: Principled Analysis of Deep Reinforcement Learning Evaluation and Design Paradigms
papers.abstract: Abstract  Starting from the utilization of deep neural networks to approximate the state-action value function that led to winning one of the most challenging games, to algorithmic advancements that allowed solving problems without even explicitly stating the rules of the challenge at hand, reinforcement learning research has been the center of remarkable scientific progress for the past decade. In this paper, we focus on the key ingredients of this research progress and we analyze the canonical evaluation and design paradigms in reinforcement learning. We introduce the theoretical foundations of scaling laws in reinforcement learning and show that the asymptotic performance of reinforcement learning algorithms does not have a monotone relationship between performance rankings and data-regimes. We conduct large-scale experiments and our results demonstrate that a line of reinforcement learning research under the canonical design and evaluation paradigms resulted in incorrect conclusions. Our analysis and results provide a core analysis on scaling, capacity and complexity of deep reinforcement learning.

ru.title: Переоценённые парадигмы: как масштабирование меняет выводы в обучении с подкреплением
ru.desc: В статье анализируются основные парадигмы оценки и разработки алгоритмов обучения с подкреплением, которые привели к значительному прогрессу в решении сложных задач. Авторы исследуют теоретические основы законов масштабирования в reinforcement learning и показывают, что асимптотическая производительность алгоритмов не имеет монотонной зависимости от объёма данных. Проведённые масштабные эксперименты выявили, что многие выводы, полученные при использовании канонических парадигм, оказались некорректными. Работа предоставляет критический анализ масштабирования, ёмкости и сложности глубокого обучения с подкреплением.

en.title: Unraveling the Complexities of Reinforcement Learning Performance
en.desc: This paper explores the advancements in reinforcement learning (RL) over the past decade, particularly focusing on deep neural networks used to approximate state-action value functions. It critically examines the traditional evaluation and design paradigms in RL, revealing that the performance of algorithms does not consistently correlate with the amount of data used. The authors introduce theoretical foundations regarding scaling laws in RL, highlighting the complexities involved in algorithm performance. Through extensive experiments, they demonstrate that previous research under standard paradigms may have led to misleading conclusions about RL effectiveness.

zh.title: 深度强化学习的关键分析与误区
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了深度强化学习的关键要素，分析了强化学习中的经典评估和设计范式。我们介绍了强化学习中规模法则的理论基础，并展示了强化学习算法的渐近性能与性能排名和数据模式之间并没有单调关系。通过大规模实验，我们的结果表明，在经典设计和评估范式下的强化学习研究得出了错误的结论。我们的分析和结果为深度强化学习的规模、能力和复杂性提供了核心分析。
